bids.reasoning: The opponent opened with all valueC, which is the worst possible for me (utility = 0.2 × (0.3+0.2+0.2+0.1+0.1+0.1) = 0.2). The opponent's "take it or leave it" at t=0 is clearly a bluff since we have the full negotiation window ahead.

The opponent is opening with all valueC (worst for me, utility = 0.2 each, total ~0.2) and claiming it's their "final offer" with aggressive rhetoric. My previous bid was all valueA (utility = 1.0). I'll repeat essentially the same strong bid since there's no reason to concede yet - the opponent hasn't budged at all and we're at the start of negotiations.

The opponent is making extreme threats but their behavior is consistent - they've bid all valueC (worst for me, utility ~0.2 per issue) every single time, while I've been bidding all valueA (best for me). I'll hold firm on my most important issue (issueA, weight 0.3) and make minor concessions on lower-weight issues to signal willingness to negotiate, while keeping my overall utility high.

This is still strong and signals I'm willing to concede on low-weight issues while holding my top priorities.
